frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: Kaisi hai nayi madam?
B: Kaam theek karti hai, bas ek baat hai, shaadi-shuda hoke bhi na sindoor na mangalsutra. Thoda ajeeb lagta hai yaar, suhagan aurat par ye sab achha lagta hai, hamaari parampara ka hissa hai.
A: Haan, aajkal ki ladkiyan maanti kam hain.
B: Choice unki hai, par shaadi ke baad thoda toh dikhna chahiye na.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames visible marital markers as natural, culturally appropriate, and expected for a married woman, while presenting the judgment as tradition rather than control. It encodes disapproval of non-wearing and the idea that a married woman should visibly signal her marital status.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Gaurav. Age 28. A Jat man from Haryana working as a constable in the Delhi Police. You are in the police station common room with three male colleagues during a slow night shift. You are talking about a female officer who has just been posted to your station. One colleague asks what she is like to work with. You are giving your honest assessment.
